system_prompt:
You are Hermes Agent, an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. You are helpful, knowledgeable, and direct. You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

Conversation started: Saturday, April 11, 2026 07:17 PM

Provider: openrouter

You are on a text messaging communication platform, Telegram. Please do not use markdown as it does not render. You can send media files natively: to deliver a file to the user, include MEDIA:/absolute/path/to/file in your response. Images (.png, .jpg, .webp) appear as photos, audio (.ogg) sends as voice bubbles, and videos (.mp4) play inline. You can also include image URLs in markdown format ![alt](url) and they will be sent as native photos.

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.
Important: cloud sandboxes may be cleaned up, idled out, or recreated between turns. Persistent filesystem means files can resume later; it does NOT guarantee a continuously running machine or surviving background processes. Use terminal sandboxes for task work, not durable hosting.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

check_interval.description: Seconds between automatic status checks for background processes (gateway/messaging only, minimum 30). When set, I'll proactively report progress.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified when the process finishes — no polling needed. Use this for tasks that take a while (tests, builds, deployments) so you can keep working on other things in the meantime.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

[Subdirectory context discovered: /tmp/mc-docs/AGENTS.md]
# Repository Guidelines

## Project Structure & Module Organization
- `backend/`: FastAPI service. Main app code lives in `backend/app/` with API routes in `backend/app/api/`, data models in `backend/app/models/`, schemas in `backend/app/schemas/`, and service logic in `backend/app/services/`.
- `backend/migrations/`: Alembic migrations (`backend/migrations/versions/` for generated revisions).
- `backend/tests/`: pytest suite (`test_*.py` naming).
- `backend/templates/`: backend-shipped templates used by gateway flows.
- `frontend/`: Next.js app. Routes under `frontend/src/app/`, shared components under `frontend/src/components/`, utilities under `frontend/src/lib/`.
- `frontend/src/api/generated/`: generated API client; regenerate instead of editing by hand.
- `docs/`: contributor and operations docs (start at `docs/README.md`).

## Build, Test, and Development Commands
- `make setup`: install/sync backend and frontend dependencies.
- `make check`: closest CI parity run (lint, typecheck, tests/coverage, frontend build).
- `docker compose -f compose.yml --env-file .env up -d --build`: run full stack.
- Fast local loop:
  - `docker compose -f compose.yml --env-file .env up -d db`
  - `cd backend && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000`
  - `cd frontend && npm run dev`
- `make api-gen`: regenerate frontend API client (backend must be on `127.0.0.1:8000`).

## Coding Style & Naming Conventions
- Python: Black + isort + flake8 + strict mypy. Max line length is 100. Use `snake_case`.
- TypeScript/React: ESLint + Prettier. Components use `PascalCase`; variables/functions use `camelCase`.
- For intentionally unused destructured TS variables, prefix with `_` to satisfy lint config.

## Testing Guidelines
- Backend: pytest via `make backend-test`; coverage policy via `make backend-coverage` (writes `backend/coverage.xml` and `backend/coverage.json`).
- Frontend: vitest + Testing Library via `make frontend-test` (coverage in `frontend/coverage/`).
- Add or update tests whenever behavior changes.

## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines
- Follow Conventional Commits (seen in history), e.g. `feat: ...`, `fix: ...`, `docs: ...`, `test(core): ...`.
- Keep PRs focused and based on latest `master`.
- Include: what changed, why, test evidence (`make check` or targeted commands), linked issue, and screenshots/logs when UI or operator workflow changes.

## Security & Configuration Tips
- Never commit secrets. Copy from `.env.example` and keep real values in local `.env`.
- Report vulnerabilities privately via GitHub security advisories, not public issues.

I now have a comprehensive understanding of the entire frontend. Here is my detailed catalog:

---

## COMPREHENSIVE FEATURE CATALOG: Mission Control Frontend

### 1. LANDING PAGE (/)
- Simple marketing landing with `LandingHero` component inside a `LandingShell` template
- Entry point for unauthenticated users

### 2. DASHBOARD (/dashboard)
- **Top Metric Cards (4 KPI cards)**:
  - Online Agents (count / total)
  - Tasks In Progress (count / total)
  - Error Rate (percentage, with latest completed count)
  - Completion Speed (tasks/day over 7-day range)
- **Workload Summary Panel**: Total work items, Inbox, In Progress, In Review, Completed counts with color-coded tones
- **Throughput Summary Panel**: Completed tasks, Average throughput (/day), Error rate, Completion consistency (active days), Review backlog ratio
- **Gateway Health Panel**: Gateway status badge (online/offline/neutral), configured/connected/unavailable/issues counts
- **Pending Approvals Section**: Links to board-specific approval pages, shows task title, board name, confidence score, relative timestamp
- **Sessions Panel**: Real-time gateway session summaries showing session key, channel, model/provider, token usage (used/max/%), last-seen timestamps
- **Recent Activity Panel**: Last 8 activity events with Markdown rendering, event type labels, relative timestamps; clickable to navigate to activity feed
- Auto-refreshes: boards (30s), agents (15s), metrics (15s), activity (15s), gateway statuses (15s)

### 3. BOARDS LIST (/boards)
- **BoardsTable**: Sortable table (by name, group, updated_at) with URL-persisted sorting
- **CRUD**: Create board button (admin only), delete board with confirmation dialog (optimistic UI updates)
- Empty state with CTA to create first board
- Shows board count

### 4. BOARD DETAIL (/boards/[boardId])
This is the most complex page (~4700 lines). Features:
- **Kanban Task Board** (`TaskBoard` component): 4 columns (Inbox, In Progress, Review, Done) with drag-and-drop between columns, FLIP animation for card movements
- **Task Cards** (`TaskCard` component): Shows title, priority badge (High/Medium/Low), assignee, due date (with overdue detection), tags (colored dots, max 3 visible), pending approval indicator (amber), blocked indicator (rose), "Waiting for lead review" indicator (indigo), draggable
- **Review column sub-filters**: All, Approval Needed, Waiting Lead, Blocked
- **Task Detail Dialog**: Edit task (title, description, status, priority, assignee, due date, tags via multi-select dropdown), delete task, custom field values editor
- **Task Comments Panel**: Real-time SSE streaming of comments, post new comments with Markdown support, highlighted comment from URL
- **Board Chat Panel** (toggleable): Real-time SSE streaming of board memory/chat messages, `BoardChatComposer` with @mention autocomplete (agents, "lead", "all"), Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline
- **Live Feed Panel** (toggleable): Real-time event stream showing task comments, task created/updated/status changes, board chat/commands, agent online/offline/created/updated, approval created/updated/approved/rejected -- each with color-coded event type pills
- **Board Goal Panel**: Shows board type (Goal vs General), objective, success metrics, target date, confirmed status, "Start onboarding" button
- **Agents Panel**: Shows agent list with status dots, pause/resume agents dialog
- **Dependency Banner**: Shows board dependencies
- **Approvals inline**: Approve/reject pending approvals from task detail
- **View modes**: Board (Kanban) and List views
- **Toast notifications**: Error/success toasts with auto-dismiss
- **Access control**: Read-only vs write access based on org membership and board-level permissions
- **Create Task Dialog**: Title, description, priority, status, assignee (from agent list), due date, tags, custom field values
- **Real-time via SSE**: Tasks stream, agents stream, approvals stream, board memory stream, activity events -- all with exponential backoff reconnection

### 5. BOARD EDIT (/boards/[boardId]/edit)
- **Board settings form**: Name, description, gateway (searchable select), board group (searchable select), board type, objective, success metrics, target date
- **Approval controls**: Require approval for done, require review before done, comment required for review, block status changes with pending approval, only lead can change status
- **Max agents setting**
- **Webhooks management**: Create/edit/delete webhooks with description, recipient agent selection, copy endpoint URL, view payloads
- **Board Onboarding Chat** (dialog, auto-opened with ?onboarding=1): Interactive Q&A chat with AI assistant for board setup, multi-choice questions with free-text option, extra context input, draft goal confirmation

### 6. BOARD APPROVALS (/boards/[boardId]/approvals)
- **BoardApprovalsPanel**: Full approval management for a single board
- Approval cards with: action type, status badge (approved/rejected/pending), confidence score with color coding, created/resolved timestamps
- Approve/Reject buttons for pending approvals
- Rubric breakdown with PieChart visualization (recharts)
- Payload details (reasoning, risk_analysis, risk_score, recommendations, evidence, concerns)
- Linked task with navigation

### 7. NEW BOARD (/boards/new)
- Form: Board name (required), gateway (required, searchable select), board group (optional, searchable select), description (required)
- Auto-slug generation, auto-redirect to edit page with onboarding flag on success
- Admin-only access

### 8. AGENTS LIST (/agents)
- **AgentsTable**: Sortable by name, status, session ID, board, last_seen_at, updated_at
- Shows: agent name (linked), status pill, session key (truncated), board link, last seen, actions
- CRUD: Create new agent, delete with confirmation dialog (optimistic updates)
- Admin-only access, 15s auto-refresh

### 9. AGENT DETAIL (/agents/[agentId])
- **Overview card**: Name, status pill, agent ID, session key, linked board, last seen, updated, created
- **Health card**: Heartbeat window, session binding (Bound/Unbound), status
- **Activity panel**: Filtered activity events for this agent with Markdown rendering
- Edit and Delete actions

### 10. AGENT EDIT (/agents/[agentId]/edit)
- **Basic config**: Agent name, role, board (searchable select with clear), emoji (dropdown with glyph preview)
- **Gateway main agent checkbox**: Detaches from single board
- **Personality & behavior**: Communication style
- **Schedule**: Heartbeat interval (e.g., 10m, 30m, 2h)
- Identity profile merge on save

### 11. NEW AGENT (/agents/new)
- Same form fields as edit: name, role, board, emoji, communication style, heartbeat interval
- Default identity profile applied
- Admin-only access

### 12. ACTIVITY FEED (/activity)
- **Global real-time activity feed** across ALL boards via SSE
- Streams: tasks (comments, created, updated, status changes), board chat/commands, agent events, approval events
- **FeedCard component**: Author avatar, title (linked), event type pill (color-coded), board name link, actor name/role, timestamp, Markdown message content
- URL-based event highlighting (scroll-to and ring highlight)
- Pagination: up to 300 feed items, paged loading (200 per page, max 1000)
- Resolves authors from agent profiles with role display

### 13. GLOBAL APPROVALS (/approvals)
- Aggregates approvals from ALL boards
- Uses the same `BoardApprovalsPanel` with `boardId="global"`
- Board label mapping for cross-board display
- Approve/reject with optimistic cache updates
- Warning aggregation for partial load failures

### 14. BOARD GROUPS LIST (/board-groups)
- **BoardGroupsTable**: Sortable by name, updated_at
- CRUD: Create group, delete with confirmation (optimistic)
- Empty state with CTA

### 15. BOARD GROUP DETAIL (/board-groups/[groupId])
- **Cross-board snapshot view**: Shows all boards in the group with their task counts and task previews
- Task status pills (Inbox, In Progress, Review, Done) with priority indicators
- **Group Chat panel** (toggleable): Real-time SSE streaming, `BoardChatComposer` with @mention support including @all, broadcast toggle
- **Notes panel** (toggleable): Separate from chat, SSE streaming, notes history
- **Heartbeat management**: Preset intervals (30s to 1h), apply heartbeat to all agents in group, include board leads toggle
- **Filters**: Include/exclude done tasks, per-board task limit control
- Board-level write access checks

### 16. NEW BOARD GROUP (/board-groups/new)
- Form: Group name (required), description, board selection with search/filter and checkbox list
- Shows "currently grouped" badge for already-grouped boards
- Assigns selected boards to group on creation

### 17. GATEWAYS LIST (/gateways)
- **GatewaysTable**: Sortable by name, workspace_root, updated_at
- CRUD: Create gateway, delete with confirmation
- Admin-only access

### 18. GATEWAY DETAIL (/gateways/[gatewayId])
- **Connection card**: Online/offline indicator, gateway URL, masked token, device pairing status
- **Runtime card**: Workspace root, created/updated timestamps
- **Agents panel**: AgentsTable filtered to this gateway's agents with delete capability
- Gateway status polling (15s)

### 19. NEW GATEWAY (/gateways/new)
- **GatewayForm**: Name, URL (with validation), token, workspace root, device pairing toggle, insecure TLS toggle
- **Connection check**: Validates gateway connectivity before creation with status indicator (checking/success/error)
- Admin-only access

### 20. TAGS (/tags)
- **TagsTable**: Sortable by name, task_count, updated_at
- CRUD: New tag, edit (navigates to /tags/[id]/edit), delete with confirmation
- Shows tag count, admin-only management

### 21. CUSTOM FIELDS (/custom-fields)
- **CustomFieldsTable**: Sortable by field_key, required, updated_at
- CRUD: Add field, edit, delete with confirmation
- **CustomFieldForm**: Field key, label, field type (select from options), visibility options, required toggle, description, string validation, board assignment (checkbox list with search)
- Organization-level fields that appear on tasks
- Admin-only access

### 22. SKILLS MARKETPLACE (/skills/marketplace)
- **MarketplaceSkillsTable**: Sortable by name, category, risk, source, updated_at
- **Filters**: Search, category filter (dynamic from data), risk level filter (Safe through Critical with sort order), pack filter
- **Pagination**: Page size options (25/50/100/200), URL-synced page/limit/search/category/risk params
- **SkillInstallDialog**: Install/uninstall skill on specific gateways, shows installed status per gateway
- Gateway-aware: skills loaded per gateway
- Admin-only access, 15s refresh

### 23. SKILL PACKS (/skills/packs)
- **SkillPacksTable**: Sortable by name, source_url, branch, skill_count, updated_at
- CRUD: Add pack, edit, delete with confirmation
- **Sync operations**: Sync individual pack, "Sync all" button (sequential to avoid rate limits)
- Sync warnings display
- Admin-only access

### 24. ORGANIZATION (/organization)
- **Organization overview**: Name, member count
- **Members & Invites Table** (`MembersInvitesTable`): Lists members and pending invites
- **Invite dialog**: Email, role selection, board access configuration
- **Board Access Editor**: Toggle between "All boards" and "Selected boards" scope, per-board read/write checkboxes (`BoardAccessTable`)
- **Member access dialog**: Edit role, edit board access (all/custom), per-board read/write
- **Remove member**: Confirmation dialog with API call
- **Revoke invites**: Cancel pending invitations with copy invite link
- **Delete organization**: Owner-only destructive action with confirmation
- Role-based visibility (owner/admin/member)

### 25. SETTINGS (/settings)
- **Profile section**: Name, timezone (searchable select from IANA timezones), email (read-only from Clerk)
- Save/Reset buttons
- **Delete account section**: Permanent deletion with confirmation dialog, signs out via Clerk

### 26. ONBOARDING (/onboarding)
- First-time user setup: Name and timezone (required)
- Auto-redirects to dashboard if onboarding already complete
- Pre-populates from Clerk user data

### 27. INVITE (/invite)
- Invite acceptance page: Paste token or use ?token= URL param
- Accept invite to join organization
- Auto-redirects to /organization on success

### KEY COMPONENTS CATALOG:

- **DashboardSidebar**: Navigation grouped into Overview (Dashboard, Live Feed), Boards (Board Groups, Boards, Tags, Approvals, Custom Fields), Skills (Marketplace, Packs -- admin only), Administration (Organization, Gateways, Agents -- admin only). System health indicator at bottom.
- **TaskBoard**: Kanban with FLIP animations, drag-and-drop, 4 status columns, review sub-filters
- **TaskCard**: Rich card with priority, assignee, due date, tags, approval/blocked/review indicators
- **BoardApprovalsPanel**: Approval management with pie charts, rubric scoring, approve/reject actions
- **BoardOnboardingChat**: AI-driven board setup wizard with multi-choice Q&A and draft goal confirmation
- **BoardChatComposer**: Text input with @mention autocomplete, keyboard navigation for mentions
- **BoardGoalPanel**: Board objective/metrics/target date display with goal vs general board distinction
- **ActivityFeed**: Generic feed renderer with loading/error/empty states
- **AgentsTable**: Data table with sorting, actions, status pills, board links
- **GatewayForm**: Reusable gateway create/edit form with connection validation
- **MarketplaceSkillForm**: Skill source URL form with GitHub URL validation
- **SkillInstallDialog**: Per-gateway skill install/uninstall dialog
- **CustomFieldForm**: Field definition form with type/visibility/validation/board assignment

### CROSS-CUTTING PATTERNS:
- **Authentication**: Clerk-based (SignedIn/SignedOut/useAuth), admin role checks via organization membership
- **Real-time**: SSE (Server-Sent Events) with exponential backoff reconnection for tasks, agents, approvals, board memory, activity
- **Data fetching**: TanStack React Query with generated API client (Orval), auto-refresh intervals (15-30s)
- **Optimistic updates**: List delete mutations with rollback on failure
- **URL-based state**: Sorting, filters, pagination, selected task/comment all synced to URL params
- **Markdown rendering**: Throughout comments, chat messages, activity events, descriptions
- **Confirm dialogs**: `ConfirmActionDialog` pattern for all destructive actions
- **Access control**: Board-level read/write permissions, org role-based admin checks, admin-only sections
